reviews.text: This app is absolute garbage! It is unreliable, flaky, loses data constantly, and is unusable on Android. It is an absolute travesty that this company foists this garbage service onto local cities and has the gall to charge them for it. This entire app needs to be put into the wood chipper and done over from scratch by a company that actually knows how to design android apps properly!
Better app. Thanks to Supervisor Lloyd Pareira
Easily report and follow up with small problems...Very Happy

You can report, you can take pictures.You can even take video.It will still take them days weeks even months to get something taken care of. city employees move like molasses. i even had an officer.Tell me that it is legal to park, unregistered and uninsured vehicles on public streets. lmao 🤣 🤣 back when I was fifteen I learned that you cannot park a unregistered and uninsured vehicle on public streets. always ask for a supervisor.

The service behind the app is incredible, but the mobile app is outdated in many ways, producing what I consider a horrendous experience. It can get worse if your city has a custom version that isn't maintained as well as the main app. It's like this on iOS, too.
Works to get the city's attention. Useful
it's a fantastic app for neighborhood busybodies who like harassing their neighbors over trivial nonsense.

There is NO CURRENT REQUESTS!!! in fact it only has FOUR, YES 4 COMPLAINTS IN TOTAL LISTED CURRENTLY. October 21 2024 is today's date. An the four that are listed are from MAY '23!!!! I thought the app needed an update but there is non. If this app was up to date an current I would consider at best a 4 star review. If fixed I will consider revising this.

Poor app 1. I cannot use the app offline as no information is stored on the phone. So when I spot a problem while out of reception this cannot be noted and prepared for uploading. 2. Limited items. Where is the "other" category? I want to report a problem that does not fit into one of the categories. "Other" should be compulsory. 3. Why can I not unsubscribe from the mailing list in total? Only specific items? This violates our local laws. 4. Why only 500 characters in this field?

The app works fine, would be better if the city actually did anything. I reported sand in the road a half a dozen times now and they keep closing the ticket without doing anything about it. Time to go back to good ol' sue the city when the road conditions cause a crash or damage to your vehicle.
Efficient
Wow. It actually works

The location finder needs work. I downloaded this specifically to report a missing concrete cover in front of a bus bench. Somebody will break an ankle stepping into it. The location finder insisted I was in Colton when I was on Baseline in San Bernardino. Then the app stated the part of Baseline I was at was not in their jurisdiction. Am uninstalling the app. I did not realize 92410 is not part of the county. Am sure you will figure out the hazard location when an ambulance gets the call.

O M G.. The number of emails they send is ridiculous. For every single thing people report in the city.. I reported one thing, and it got fixed, so the functionality is good. I apprec that it works. They just need to chill on the crazy amount of emails they send

What started off as a good idea has transitioned into an app where all the "Karen's" of the world can have a safe space to complain about people less fortunate then them. Thanks to this app, the lives of thousands of unhoused/homeless people are constantly being challenged and harassed for being homeless. It's understandable that we have a problem with our homeless epidemic, it's only going to get worse. Instead of harassing homeless people, let's figure out a solution that works for everyone.

Though this app is an improvement over my city's previous reporting systems, it's still far from perfect. SeeClickFix staff need to work on their apps' UI, which could be made more aesthetically appealing, less clunky, and more intuitive. Also helpful would be the ability to copy text within individual request pages, and to suggest that a request recently closed by an organization be reopened if within a certain timeframe (where users cannot already reopen a request themselves).

App for neighbors to whine about stuff they could just be humans and deal with on their own. This app almost got my camper van stolen. While the idea for this app is interesting the application is horrific. Some residents use the app to report serious issues but at least 50% of all posts are whining about neighbors who park poorly or about a bottle on the sidewalk they want some one else to come and throw away for them or pictures of people's homes under and overpass listed as "debris". This app needs the ability for neighbors to come together to improve our own neighborhoods. Or it needs to partner directly with ways to get involved in making our community better. Please don't install this destructive, hate-first app. I parked my Camper Van on my street a few doors down from my hose late on Sunday. I could not get the van to its permanent place until the following weekend. I was careful to ensure it was parked well and parked legally. It was reported as abandoned and two hrs after being reported I caught two people try to break in. They told me it was reported on an abandoned car site to justify their actions. Their MUST be a better way to do this than anonymous snitching! Don't install this app. Just be a good person.

Nice idea, poorly implemented. Keep trying To the response: Use the app. Test the app. Try the app. Hard to navigate Hard to close open tickets Cant find things small text boxes for data entry Its just sucks And a great idea all at the same time Seems designed by someone that has never used the app.

Great app for getting the county to fix issues. Couple tips. 1. Create a special email so you don't get retaliation from the gov't or the citizen you are reporting. You also can choose to post as "anonymous" but if you forget, your email is revealed. 2. Get the date time stamp camera mobile app. It can put a date time location compass and map right on the photos, saving a lot of time.

Android version of this app simply doesn't work. I haven't yet managed to file a report - it's always a "failed network connection," a perpetually spinning circle when I try to upload a photo, or a plain old crash. I use the web version often with no trouble at all, but the mobile app is so far a real stinker. I uninstalled it due to its unreliability and to the developers' failure to fix this ridiculous flaw: Users can't follow up (comment) on a complaint anonymously or as a guest.
